sourceMovie.title: Eye in the Sky
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Eye in the Sky works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Hurt Locker
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Eye in the Sky clicked for you, The Hurt Locker (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Eye in the Sky?
faq.answer: Try The Hurt Locker, The Wall, Body of Lies—each shares DNA with Eye in the Sky while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Eye in the Sky?
faq.answer: Eye in the Sky (2015) is best described as Drama, Thriller, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Eye in the Sky worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
